A car company has three sales people, Anna, Mustapha and Joshua.

(a) During March, Anna sold 21 cars, Mustapha sold 12 cars and Joshua sold 15 cars.
Write down and simplify the ratio of the number of cars they sold during March.

Anna : Mustapha : Joshua =  :  : 

(b) Each month, they receive a bonus which is proportional to the number of cars they sell.
The total bonus in March is \$1248.
(i) (c) Show that Anna receives a bonus of \$546.

(d) Calculate the bonuses received by Mustapha and Joshua.

Mustapha \$ \\
Joshua \$ 

(e) The total bonus of \$1248 is $\dfrac{1}{7}$ of the total profit in March.
Calculate the total profit in March.

\$ 

(f) Ella wants to buy a car with a price of \$13 500.
The company reduces this price by 16\%.
Ella then pays a deposit of \$500.
Show that the amount left for her to pay is \$10 840.

(g) Ella borrows \$10 840 from a bank.
She pays this back over 3 years at a rate of \$340 per month.
(i) (h) Show that the total amount she pays back during the 3 years is \$12 240.

(i) Calculate the percentage increase from \$10 840 to \$12 240.

 \%

tex:
The scale drawing represents three sides, AB, BC and CD, of a wildlife park.
The scale is 1 centimetre represents 50 metres.

% \begin{InsertScreenShot}
%     
% \end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Find the actual distance AB in metres.

(b) Point E is 550 metres from A and 600 metres from D.
Use a ruler and compasses only to find the point E and draw the lines AE and DE.

(c) Two straight paths cross the wildlife park, ABCDE.
Using a straight edge and compasses only, construct
(i) (d) the path that bisects angle ABC,

(e) the path that is equidistant from point C and point D.

(f) The path from B crosses over a circular lake with radius 150 m.
The centre of the lake is on this path and is 350 m from B.
(i) (g) On the scale drawing, construct the lake.

(h) Calculate the actual circumference of the lake in metres.

 & French & German & Spanish & Italian & Japanese & Total \\

\hline
Total & 53 &  & 54 &  &  & 262 \\
\hline

(a) Complete the table.

(b) Find the probability that
(i) (c) a girl, chosen at random, studies Spanish,

(d) a boy, chosen at random, studies French or Italian,

(e) a student, chosen at random, does not study German.

(f) 72 students each study one of the sciences shown in the table.
The results are to be shown in a pie chart.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Science & Number of students & Pie chart sector angle \\
\hline
Biology & 25 & 125° \\
\hline
Chemistry & 16 &  \\
\hline
Physics & 31 &  \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (g) Complete the table.

(h) Complete the pie chart.

tex:
Louise leaves home at 09 55 and cycles the 5.6 km to the supermarket at a constant speed.
She takes 15 minutes to complete the journey.

(a) Write down the time she arrives at the supermarket.

(b) Calculate Louise's average speed from her home to the supermarket
(i) (c) in kilometres per hour,

(d) in metres per second, giving your answer correct to 1 decimal place.

(e) Louise stays at the supermarket for 23 minutes.
On the grid opposite, draw the travel graph of her journey from home and her stay at the supermarket.

(f) Louise's mother leaves home at 10 07 to meet Louise at the supermarket.
She cycles at a constant speed of 28 km/h.
(i) (g) Work out how long she takes for the 5.6 km journey.
Give your answer in minutes.

(h) On the grid, show her mother's journey.

(i) They cycle home together at a constant speed and arrive at 10 54.
(i) (j) On the grid, show their journey home.

(k) Calculate, in km/h, their constant speed on the journey home.
